title: Recursion: Build the Full Tree
description: Learn how decision trees repeat the split search on each impure child until the tree is complete.
takeaways: After the root split, impure child nodes are split again using the same process.
Pure child nodes become leaves with a class prediction.
Recursion continues until all leaves are pure or stopping rules apply.

practiceLab.objective: Build a tiny decision tree recursively with pure-node, min_samples_split, and max_depth base cases, then inspect left and right children.
instructions: Run the dataset cell for the eight-row age/bp table.
Run the helper cell that defines gini, weighted_gini, and best_split.
Run the guided build_tree cell with max_depth=2 and print the nested tree dictionary.
Complete Exercise 1 by implementing describe(tree) to print nodes and leaves.
Confirm indented output shows a root split plus left/right subtrees or leaves.
Complete Exercise 2 by rebuilding with max_depth=1 and printing the shallower tree.
Compare which base-case reasons appear on the leaves.
Answer the reflection questions about depth limits and child creation.
hints: Base cases: pure labels, len(frame) < min_samples_split, or depth >= max_depth.
After a split, call build_tree on both left and right subsets with depth+1.
describe should recurse into node["left"] and node["right"].
practiceLab.expectedOutput: A nested tree dict for max_depth=2, an indented describe printout, and a shallower max_depth=1 tree printout.
